resetDialog.message: Are you sure you want to reset "{{name}}"? All rounds, pairings and results will be cleared. Players and settings will be kept. Format lock will be released.

createDialog.title: Create New Tournament
createDialog.subtitle: Just enter a name to get started. All other settings can be changed later.

players.emptyHint: Click the button above to add the first player
players.deleteMsg: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
players.importHint: Found {{count}} rows. Please map the fields below.
players.importFailed: Import failed, please check file format
players.importNoName: Please select a column for the Name field
players.lockedBanner: Tournament has started. Adding or removing players is disabled. Use Withdraw to adjust.

rounds.emptyHint: Click the button below to start the first round of pairing

error.tooFewPlayers: At least 2 active players are required

rounds.undoMessage: Are you sure you want to undo Round {{round}}? All pairings and results for this round will be removed.
rounds.undoAllMessage: Are you sure you want to clear the full schedule for "{{name}}"? All rounds and results will be removed. Players will be kept.
rounds.clearResultsMessage: Are you sure you want to clear all results for Round {{round}}? Pairings will be kept and you can re-enter results.
rounds.halfByeHint: Selected players will sit out the next round and receive draw points (½).
rounds.halfByeDesc: Half-point bye is for players who cannot participate in a round. Unlike the system bye (full point, auto-assigned for odd numbers), a half-point bye is requested voluntarily and only awards draw points, reflecting the cost of absence.
rounds.halfByeCompare: System Bye = Full point (win)  |  Half-Point Bye = Half point (draw)

rankings.title: Rankings
rankings.emptyHint: Rankings will appear after the first round results are recorded

toast.restoreFailed: Restore failed, please check the file format
toast.loadFailed: Failed to load tournament data. The file may be missing or corrupted.

settings.formatLocked: Tournament has started. To change format, reset the tournament first.

formatProcess.swiss: Each round pairs players with similar scores. No repeated matchups. Final ranking by total score.
formatProcess.round_robin: Every player plays every other player once. Final ranking by total score.
formatProcess.double_round_robin: Every player plays every other player twice (once per color). Final ranking by total score.
formatProcess.elimination: Losers are eliminated each round. Winners advance until a champion is decided.
formatProcess.double_elimination: Winners and losers brackets. First loss sends you to losers bracket; second loss eliminates.

formatScenario.swiss: Best for large groups with limited rounds
formatScenario.round_robin: Best for small groups where fairness is priority
formatScenario.double_round_robin: Best for small groups needing color balance in chess
formatScenario.elimination: Best for quick results and spectator appeal
formatScenario.double_elimination: Best when players deserve a second chance

settings.totalRoundsAuto: Leave empty to auto-calculate based on player count (⌈log₂(n)⌉), or set manually
settings.totalRoundsFormula: Auto-calculation uses ⌈log₂(n)⌉ — the same number of rounds as a knockout tournament, guaranteeing a sole leader. Players vs. rounds:
settings.colorBalanceDesc: Balance white/black assignments for each player. Recommended for chess.
settings.bronzeMatchDesc: Add a match between the two semifinal losers to determine 3rd and 4th place.
settings.bracketResetDesc: If losers bracket champion wins Game 1, play a deciding Game 2 (since winners bracket champion has 0 losses).
settings.allowDrawDesc: When disabled, every game must have a decisive result. Suitable for Go (with komi), elimination events, etc.
settings.tiebreakersDesc: When scores are tied, these criteria are applied in order of priority.

tbDesc.bhc1: Sum of all opponents' scores, dropping the lowest one
tbDesc.bh: Sum of all opponents' scores — stronger opponents mean a higher value
tbDesc.bhc2: Sum of all opponents' scores, dropping the two lowest
tbDesc.bhm1: Sum of all opponents' scores, dropping the highest and lowest
tbDesc.sb: Full credit for wins, half for draws, none for losses — weighted by opponent strength
tbDesc.progressive: Running total of scores after each round — early wins count more
tbDesc.wins: Total number of games won (draws don't count)
tbDesc.koya: Score against opponents who achieved 50%+ of max points — for round robin
tbDesc.black: Total games played as black (second player) — more is better

tbMeaning.bhc1: Stronger opponents mean higher quality results; dropping the lowest removes first-round luck
tbMeaning.bh: Complements Cut-1 with a complete picture of opponent strength
tbMeaning.bhc2: Better for large events — dropping two extremes gives more stable results
tbMeaning.bhm1: Removes both the strongest and weakest outliers for a fairer middle ground
tbMeaning.de: When tied players met each other, the winner deserves the higher rank
tbMeaning.sb: Distinguishes beating strong opponents from beating weak ones — measures win quality
tbMeaning.progressive: Rewards players who performed consistently well from the start
tbMeaning.wins: Among equal scores, more decisive wins show a more aggressive playing style
tbMeaning.koya: Only counts results against strong opponents — ideal for round robin evaluation
tbMeaning.black: Playing black (second) is a natural disadvantage — more black games deserve compensation

settings.avoidancesDesc: Specify player pairs that should not be matched against each other during pairing.

intro.title: About SaiPair
intro.desc: SaiPair is a free, offline-first desktop tournament management software. It helps organizers with player pairing, schedule arrangement, result recording, and ranking calculation.
intro.features: Supported formats: Swiss, Round Robin, Double Round Robin, Single Elimination, Double Elimination. Features include flexible scoring rules, multiple tiebreak criteria, Excel/CSV import & export, print support, and more.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.step1: Create a tournament — click "Create New Tournament" on the home page, enter a name to get started. Format defaults to Swiss; all settings can be changed later.
quickStart.step2: Add players — go to the Players tab to add players one by one, or import from an Excel/CSV file in bulk.
quickStart.step3: Generate pairings — go to the Rounds tab, click "Start Pairing" to generate the first round. The system pairs players automatically based on the selected format.
quickStart.step4: Record results — click each pairing row to enter the result. Once all results are recorded, you can generate the next round.
quickStart.step5: View rankings — go to the Rankings tab. Rankings update in real time based on scores and tiebreak rules. You can also print or export results.

settings.title: Tournament Settings
settings.format: Format: Choose from Swiss, Round Robin, Double Round Robin, Single Elimination, or Double Elimination. Once the first round is generated, the format is locked. To change it, reset the tournament first.
settings.scoring: Scoring: Configure points for win, draw, and loss. Default is 2-1-0 (stored as integers internally, displayed divided by 2).
settings.tiebreakers: Tiebreak rules: When players have the same score, tiebreak criteria are applied in priority order. Supported: Buchholz (BH, BH-C1, BH-C2, BH-M1), Sonneborn-Berger, Progressive Score, Direct Encounter, Number of Wins, Koya System, Number of Black Games.

players.title: Player Management
players.desc: Add players manually or import from Excel/CSV. Each player has fields: name (required), rating, organization, gender, birth date, title, phone, and notes. After the tournament starts (first round generated), you cannot add or delete players — use the Withdraw function instead. Withdrawn players are excluded from future pairings but their past results are preserved.

rounds.title: Rounds & Pairings
rounds.desc: Click "Start Pairing" or "Generate Next Round" to create pairings. The system automatically pairs players based on the format rules. Click a pairing row to record the result (win/draw/loss/forfeit). You can undo the current round's pairings, clear results, or undo all rounds. For elimination formats, a bracket view is available.

rankings.title: Rankings & Export
rankings.desc: Rankings are calculated in real time from scores and tiebreak rules — no manual action needed. You can print player rosters, ranking tables, cross tables, individual record cards, and pairing sheets. Export to Excel is also supported. For Swiss tournaments, TRF format export is available for interoperability with other systems.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: Can I change the format after the tournament starts?
faq.a1: No. Once the first round is generated, the format is locked. You can reset the tournament (clears all rounds, keeps players) to unlock the format selection.
faq.q2: What happens when a player withdraws?
faq.a2: Withdrawn players are excluded from future pairings. Their past results remain in the records. Opponents who played against a withdrawn player keep their results. You can restore a withdrawn player at any time.
faq.a3: Primary ranking is by total score. When scores are tied, tiebreak rules are applied in the order you configured. You can add, remove, or reorder tiebreak criteria in tournament settings.
faq.a4: All data is stored locally on your computer as JSON files. You can back up a tournament to a .saipair file and restore it on another computer. No internet connection is required.

about.title: About
about.version: SaiPair — Free, offline-first tournament management software.
about.feedback: Feedback & support: Please visit saipair.com for contact information and to report issues.
